title: Upon Himself
content:
Thou shalt not all die; for while Love's fire shines
    Upon his altar, men shall read thy lines;
    And learn'd musicians shall, to honour Herrick's
    Fame, and his name, both set and sing his lyrics.

    To his book's end this last line he'd have placed:--
    Jocund his Muse was, but his Life was chaste.
meaning:
"Upon Himself" is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
